payload.summary: All four amendments identify genuine correctness, safety, or maintainability issues in the codebase. The silent contract violation in unique_terminal_replay_match hinders production debugging. The missing merge coverage test for TaskRunEntry leaves the same drift-prone gap already addressed for journal details. The plan_hash auto-population race is documented in-code but still produces permanently incorrect lineage. The None-backfill semantic mismatch can corrupt legacy entries created under earlier plan versions.

votes.rationale: The contract check at fs.rs:2837 silently returns None on a violated invariant (run_id supplied without started_at) with no diagnostic output. The original spec explicitly requires runtime checks that return errors in both debug and release builds. While callers do eventually surface an error when None is returned, the specific reason for the contract violation — which is critical for diagnosing why the invariant was broken — is lost. Adding a tracing::warn! or tracing::error! before the return is minimal cost and high diagnostic value. This is a straightforward improvement.

TaskRunEntry::merge_attempt_entries at model.rs:320 uses the exact same manual field-by-field merge pattern that was already identified as drift-prone for journal details (issue 1), and the codebase already has the analogous test journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields at fs.rs:3922. The merge function handles run_id, plan_hash, snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation, outcome_detail, started_at, and finished_at — any future optional field addition that omits a merge line would silently drop data. A round-trip coverage test following the established pattern is a cheap guard against this exact class of regression.

The code at fs.rs:3034-3055 auto-populates plan_hash from the current snapshot hash when the caller omits it. The in-code comment at lines 3037-3042 explicitly documents the race: if persist_plan() runs between dispatch and record_task_run_start, the entry is stamped with the newer snapshot hash instead of the plan version that actually dispatched the bead. This produces permanently incorrect lineage and journal data. Documenting a known bug does not neutralize it — the auto-population path can silently corrupt data. The comment's own recommendation ('callers should always supply plan_hash explicitly') confirms the current fallback is unsafe. Either the fallback should be removed (forcing callers to supply plan_hash), or a dispatch-time plan_hash capture mechanism should be introduced.

The semantic mismatch is real and consequential. model.rs:286-290 documents None as 'legacy entries that predate this field,' but fs.rs:2608-2615 interprets None as 'created before any plan existed' and immediately backfills the current snapshot hash into both snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation and plan_hash. These are not equivalent: a legacy entry created under plan V1 (before the snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation field was added) will have None, and replaying or completing it after the snapshot advances to V2 will silently relabel it with V2. This corrupts lineage for exactly the legacy rows the provenance guard was meant to protect. The fix should either distinguish 'no field existed' from 'no plan existed' (e.g., via a sentinel value or separate migration flag), or refuse to backfill when the entry is already in a non-initial state.
